testcase: Missing PR links in Release Planner
query:
title: Missing PR links in Release Planner

question: We have two services for which we have active release planners but are running into issues with each:

For the first one, we are transitioning our SDKs from Swagger to Typespec and created a new release planner. [Management-Plane SDK Release App - Power Apps](https://apps.powerapps.com/play/e/ed2ffefd-774d-40dd-ab23-7fff01aeec9f/a/bec76eb9-3c3f-43db-b115-d5084bf488b9?workflow=38259991-fb29-f111-8341-000d3a5b007c&tenantId=72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47#legacyredirected) - due to some missing .NET emitter config, there is no linked .NET SDK PR. Do we need to regenerate the SDKs after adding the missing .NET emitter config? Will it overwrite the existing PRs that have been merged for other languages?

For the second service - [Management-Plane SDK Release App - Power Apps](https://apps.powerapps.com/play/e/ed2ffefd-774d-40dd-ab23-7fff01aeec9f/a/bec76eb9-3c3f-43db-b115-d5084bf488b9?workflow=3d3b63e5-bc2c-f111-88b4-000d3a597585&tenantId=72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47#legacyredirected) - multiple SDK generation PRs were created and the linked PRs are incorrect for two of the languages. The correct PRs are [[AutoPR azure-mgmt-relationships]-generated-from-SDK Generation - Python-6084790 by azure-sdk · Pul…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/pull/46006) for Python and [Generate new namespace and SDK for Relationships resource type by meghanagupta95 · Pull Request #57…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/57520) for .NET. Is it possible to link these to the release planner and move ahead with releasing the SDKs?
ground_truth:
You may use SDK Tools Agent to regenerate .NET SDK specifically, then link it back to the release plan.
[Azure SDK Tools Agent | Azure SDK](https://eng.ms/docs/products/azure-developer-experience/develop/azsdk-tools-mcp)

expected_references.title: Azure SDK Tools Agent | Azure SDK

testcase: Not able to do the release as Release planner is not getting updated
query:
title: Not able to do the release as Release planner is not getting updated

question: I have a release plan where the PR status isn't displaying correctly.
5/5 PR's are merged but they still show as draft. I want to trigger the release for all languages. Could someone help with this? Please let me know if I might be overlooking something.
ground_truth: We are in the process of sunsetting Release Planner. Please use our new release dashboard ([Azure SDK Release Plan Dashboard](https://azsdk-releaseplan-dashboard-hveph5aqhhcfhtgu.westus-01.azurewebsites.net/?filter=2246)) and follow the instructions there with our Azure SDK Agent.

query:
title: Releasing SDK with OpenAPI specs

question: Due to SFI, we deprecated APIs with older versions
[Remove SourceControls resource type from deprecated API versions by guywilfshukrun · Pull Request #…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/43819)
 
I want to generate new version of SDK for SecurityInsights
The current situation is that we have all stable versions in OpenAPI, and  latest preview in typespec.
From the agent docs I see that only typespec is supported.
How can I generate SDK for securityinsights with latest preview version and latest stable version? We have SDK from 2024, and didn't generate anything newer
 
Appreciate your help!
ground_truth:
I noticed that main.tsp only includes API version 2025-07-01-preview, while the latest stable version in Swagger is 2025-09-01. Does this mean a new stable version was added in Swagger after the TypeSpec conversion? That should not happen. After conversion, the service should define all new versions in TypeSpec. Please add 2025-09-01 to TypeSpec, then request SDK releases for that stable version.
 
Refer to [Adding a Stable Version when the Last Version was Preview | TypeSpec Azure](https://azure.github.io/typespec-azure/docs/howtos/versioning/03-stable-after-preview/)

expected_references.title: Adding a Stable Version when the Last Version was Preview | TypeSpec Azure

testcase: Release Plan Dashboard Not Reflecting Released SDK Package Status
query:
title: Release Plan Dashboard Not Reflecting Released SDK Package Status

question: I linked a different JS SDK PR to the release plan because another package with the same API version had already been merged and released last week: Azure SDK Release Plan Dashboard. Released package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@azure/arm-msi/v/2.3.0-beta.1, which has the same package version of the linked PR: [[AutoPR @azure-arm-msi]-generated-from-SDK Generation - JS-6319430 by azure-sdk · Pull Request #385….](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/38589/changes#diff-7098e92859fe28791786c71aa37c3576a7e12e68f746a327e0e3f821a1a693c7)
However, the “Package Link” and “Action Required” fields on the dashboard are not correctly reflected after updating the PR. How does the dashboard determine whether a package has already been released or not?
ground_truth:
Engineering systems already has automation to close any stale PRs if inactive for a specific period. It is not supported now to automatically close a PR when a release plan is abandoned.
 
Release planner dashboard does not use the PR content to identify the package version. Release status and release version is set in the release plan by release pipeline by looking up a release plan for the package name. JavaScript package @azure/arm-msi was released on 5/20 and release pipeline did not find any matching release plan for the package. Package name was updated in the release plan later on 5/21. 
 
release status is updated by the release pipeline and it won't recheck the status if there was not release plan was found during the release stage.
 
I will mark this package as Released now.
